pi_items.source: Official Sygic GPS Navigation documentation from manula.com. The feature is called 'Sharp curves' and is located in Settings > Notifications > Sharp curves. The feature name contains both keywords: 'sharp' and 'curve'.

Android SharedPreferences standard format uses 'true' and 'false' boolean values in XML. When the 'Sharp curves' notification setting is enabled in Sygic GPS Navigation, the corresponding XML preference key is set to 'true'. The task requires enabling the feature, so 'true' is the correct expected state.

privileged_info_summary: Sygic GPS Navigation (com.sygic.aura) is a real navigation application that includes a 'Sharp curves' warning notification feature. This feature is located in Settings > Notifications > Sharp curves and allows drivers to receive warnings about upcoming sharp curves on the route. The feature can be enabled or disabled via a toggle switch. When enabled, the corresponding boolean preference in the app's SharedPreferences XML file is set to 'true'. The metadata keywords 'curve' and 'sharp' accurately represent the feature name as it appears in the app's user interface. The expected_state value of 'true' correctly represents the enabled state of this boolean preference.
